etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 奧(奧) (ào) -> express an exclamation "oh"
etymology.interpretation: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") provides the semantic element as it relates to expressions or sounds made with the mouth. 奧(奧) (ào) offers the phonetic element, supplying the pronunciation. The character 噢 imitates the sound one makes to express realization or acknowledgment, such as "oh" in English, specifically borrowing the sound for this exclamatory function.

examples.chinese: 噢(噢)，我刚想起来一件重要的事情(我剛想起來一件重要的事情)。
examples.pinyin: Ō, wǒ gāng xiǎng qǐlái yī jiàn zhòngyào de shìqíng.
examples.english: Oh, I just remembered something important.

examples.chinese: 噢(噢)，原来是这样啊(原來是這樣啊)！
examples.english: Oh, so that's how it is!

examples.chinese: 噢(噢)，下雨了(下雨了)，我们带伞吧(我們帶傘吧)。
examples.pinyin: Ō, xià yǔ le, wǒmen dài sǎn ba.
examples.english: Oh, it's raining, let's bring an umbrella.
